actions/runner-images#11101.body:
### Breaking changes

We will soon start the deprecation process for Ubuntu 20.04. While the image is being deprecated, you may experience longer queue times during peak usage hours. Deprecation will begin on **2025-02-01** and the image will be fully unsupported by **2025-04-15**.

To raise awareness of the upcoming removal, we will temporarily fail jobs using Ubuntu 20.04. Builds that are scheduled to run during the brownout periods will fail. The brownouts are scheduled for the following dates and times:

**GitHub Actions\Azure DevOps::**

- March 4 14:00 UTC – 22:00 UTC
- March 11 13:00 UTC – 21:00 UTC
- March 18 13:00 UTC – 21:00 UTC
- March 25 13:00 UTC – 21:00 UTC
- April 1 13:00 UTC – 21:00 UTC
- April 8 13:00 UTC – 21:00 UTC



### Target date

~2025-04-01~
2025-04-15

### The motivation for the changes

We maintain the latest two stable versions of any given OS version. Ubuntu 24.04 is going GA on 2025-02-01 so we start deprecating the oldest image at that time.

### Possible impact

Workflows using the `ubuntu-20.04` image label should be updated to `ubuntu-latest`, `ubuntu-22.04`, `ubuntu-24.04` .

### Platforms affected

- [ ] Azure DevOps
- [x] GitHub Actions

### Runner images affected

- [x] Ubuntu 20.04
- [ ] Ubuntu 22.04
- [ ] Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] macOS 12
- [ ] macOS 13
- [ ] macOS 13 Arm64
- [ ] macOS 14
- [ ] macOS 14 Arm64
- [ ] macOS 15
- [ ] macOS 15 Arm64
- [ ] Windows Server 2019
- [ ] Windows Server 2022

### Mitigation ways

N/A
actions/runner-images#11101.title: The Ubuntu 20.04 Actions runner image will begin deprecation on 2025-02-01 and will be fully unsupported by 2025-04-15

nocodb/nocodb#10203.body:
### Please confirm that the feature request does ***not*** already exist

- [X] I confirm there is no existing issue for this feature request.

### Use case

Hello,  

I would like to suggest adding support for generating and displaying thumbnail previews of PDF files in NocoDB. This feature could significantly enhance the user experience when working with tables containing PDF attachments, as it would allow users to quickly identify and interact with their files visually.  

### Use Cases:  
- Users managing documents like invoices, contracts, or presentations would benefit greatly from this feature.  
- It would streamline workflows and reduce the time needed to locate specific PDFs.  

### Suggested solution

### Current Behavior:  
When a PDF file is attached to a table, it appears as a file icon without any preview. This lack of visual representation makes it challenging to differentiate between PDFs at a glance.  

### Expected Behavior:  
Users should be able to see a small thumbnail preview of the first page of a PDF file directly within the table view or in a dedicated preview area.  

### Suggested Implementation:  
1. Utilize an open-source library like PDF.js or other solutions to generate a thumbnail image of the first page of the PDF file.  
2. Display the generated thumbnail in the appropriate cell in the table.  
3. Optionally allow users to expand the thumbnail to view a larger version or navigate through the pages of the PDF.  

### Additional context

If this feature is currently supported in any way, I would appreciate guidance on how to enable or configure it.  

Thank you for considering this suggestion, and I look forward to your feedback!
nocodb/nocodb#10203.title: 🔦 Feature: Support for Thumbnail Previews of PDF Files in NocoDB

nocodb/nocodb#11309.body:
### Please confirm that the bug report does ***not*** already exist

- [x] I confirm there is no existing issue for this bug.

### Steps to reproduce

Some API v3 meta endpoints do not match the Swagger schema. In particular, they require the `base_id` be passed twice, which appears to be unintentional.

Calling this endpoint returns a 404 Not Found:

```
GET /api/v3/meta/bases/{base_id}/tables
```

However, calling this endpoint returns 200 OK and a valid response body:

```
GET /api/v3/meta/bases/{base_id}/bases/{base_id}/tables
```

Note we have to include `/bases/{base_id}` twice.

The issue seems to be [here](https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb/blob/4506df0ce5c620a4d3ad128791b589c38e3bafa6/packages/nocodb/src/controllers/v3/tables-v3.controller.ts#L33), where `PREFIX_APIV3_METABASE` is `/api/v3/meta/bases/:baseId`. This causes the `/bases/:baseId` to be included in the endpoint path twice. Note that this is not the only endpoint this bug affects.

### Desired Behavior

I would expect that calling this endpoint returns 200 OK and a valid response body, as documented in the Swagger schema.

```
GET /api/v3/meta/bases/{base_id}/tables
```

### Project Details

Node: **v22.15.0**
Arch: **x64**
Platform: **linux**
Docker: **false**
RootDB: **pg**
PackageVersion: **0.263.0**

nocodb/nocodb#11374.body:
### Please confirm that the feature request does ***not*** already exist

- [x] I confirm there is no existing issue for this feature request.

### Use case

In a NocoDB view, I filter records using a single-select field, such as:

- Project = A项目

- Status = 进行中

This filter limits the view to only relevant records. However, when I click “Add Record” within the view, those single-select field filters are not used as default values in the new record form. I need to manually select them again.

This is repetitive and error-prone in workflows where I frequently add data under the same filtered condition.

### Suggested solution

When creating a new record within a filtered view:

- If the filter involves single-select fields, use those selected values as default values in the add record form.

- The values should still be editable by the user.

- This should apply only to fields with filters in the current view.

Example:
If the view filter is Project = A项目 (where "Project" is a single-select), then the "Add Record" form should pre-fill the "Project" field with "A项目".

### Additional context

This feature would streamline data entry in filtered contexts (e.g. adding tasks to a specific project or adding leads under a specific campaign).
It would also align with user expectations when working with filtered views — especially for single-select fields, where the selected option has a clear and unambiguous value.
nocodb/nocodb#11374.title: 🔦 Feature: Use single-select filter values in views as default when adding new records

# Issue
Use NocoDB with a SQLite database. On version 0.263.4. I have a table that uses JSON data type. When I query the table and JSON column for records using the `like` operator. i.e. (AlertGeocode,like,040109) i receive a 400 error. I believe this broke around the time 0.263.0 came out, as this worked previously. I found #10860 that is kind of similar to this issue, but my issue is not resolved.

# Request
```
curl -X 'GET' \
  'https://db.domain.com/api/v2/tables/tableID/records?viewId=vwfj8lyoclapi6fy&where=%28AlertGeocode%2Clike%2C840%29&limit=25&shuffle=0&offset=0' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'xc-token: TOKEN'
```

# Response
```json
{
  "error": "DATABASE_ERROR",
  "message": "near",
  "code": "SQLITE_ERROR"
}
```

nocodb/nocodb#12759.body:
## Summary

The MultiSelect field still has a practical limit of approximately 500 options, despite the fix merged in PR #8907 (Feb 2025) which was supposed to remove this limitation.

## Environment

- **NocoDB Version**: 0.265.1
- **Database**: PostgreSQL (external data source)
- **Deployment**: Self-hosted (Docker)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a MultiSelect field
2. Try to add more than 500 options via API:

```python
import requests

url = f'{NOCODB_URL}/api/v2/meta/columns/{COLUMN_ID}'
payload = {
    'colOptions': {
        'options': [{'title': opt} for opt in options_list]  # 600+ options
    }
}
response = requests.patch(url, headers=headers, json=payload)
```

## Expected Behavior

Should be able to add 1000+ options to a MultiSelect field (as per the fix in #8907).

## Actual Behavior

- ✅ 500 options: Works
- ❌ 600 options: Fails with `{"error":"DATABASE_ERROR","message":"too","code":"SQLITE_ERROR"}`

## Use Case

We have a production notes system with 1,161 unique values that users need to select from. The current 500 limit forces us to either:
1. Use "Link to Another Record" (worse UX - requires 2 steps to add new values)
2. Limit options to 500 (incomplete data)

## Workaround Attempted

Using "Link to Another Record" instead of MultiSelect, but this significantly degrades user experience:
- MultiSelect: Search → Add instantly if not found
- Link: Search → Not found → Go to options table → Add record → Return → Select

## Request

Please fully remove the ~500 option limit for MultiSelect fields, or at minimum increase it to 2000+ options.

## Related Issues

- #8907 - Original fix that was supposed to remove this limit

nocodb/nocodb#12882.body:
### Please confirm that the bug report does ***not*** already exist

- [x] I confirm there is no existing issue for this bug.

### Steps to reproduce

## Bug Description

After upgrading from NocoDB 0.260.2 to 0.301.1, a Super Admin user can no longer access bases through the web interface. The user receives a `403 Forbidden` error with the message "Base is not accessible".

The issue is specific to the **v1 API endpoints**. The v2 API endpoints correctly recognize the Super Admin role and return data successfully.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have a NocoDB instance running version 0.260.2 or earlier
2. Create a Super Admin user with roles `org-level-creator,super`
3. Verify the user can access all bases via the web interface
4. Upgrade NocoDB to version 0.301.1
5. Log in with the Super Admin user
6. Attempt to access any base

## Expected Behavior

Super Admin should have access to all bases regardless of base-level permissions, as documented in NocoDB's role hierarchy.

## Actual Behavior

Super Admin receives `403 Forbidden` error when trying to access bases through the web interface.

## Technical Analysis

### API Endpoint Comparison

**v2 API (working correctly):**
```bash
curl -X GET "https://nocodb.example.com/api/v2/meta/bases" \
  -H "xc-auth: <jwt_token>"

# Response: 200 OK - Returns list of all bases
```

**v1 API (failing):**
```bash
curl -X GET "https://nocodb.example.com/api/v1/db/meta/projects/<base_id>" \
  -H "xc-auth: <jwt_token>"

# Response: 403 Forbidden
# Body: {"msg":"Base is not accessible"}
```

### User Verification

The user exists in `nc_users_v2` with correct roles:
```sql
SELECT id, email, roles FROM nc_users_v2 WHERE email = 'admin@example.com';

-- Result:
-- id: xxxxxxxxx
-- email: admin@xxxxx.com
-- roles: org-level-creator,super
```

### Root Cause Hypothesis

The ACL middleware for v1 API endpoints appears to only check base-level permissions in `nc_base_users_v2` and does not fall back to checking the org-level `super` role in `nc_users_v2.roles`.

The v2 API endpoints correctly implement this fallback, which is why they work.

## Environment

- **NocoDB Version:** 0.301.1 (Docker image: `nocodb/nocodb:0.301.1`)
- **Previous Version:** 0.260.2 (working correctly)
- **Database:** PostgreSQL 14
- **Cache:** Redis (issue persists with Redis disabled)
- **Deployment:** Docker Compose



## Workaround

As a temporary workaround, the Super Admin user can be manually added as "owner" to each base in the `nc_base_users_v2` table:

```sql
INSERT INTO nc_base_users_v2 (base_id, fk_user_id, roles, created_at, updated_at)
SELECT
    b.id,
    '<super_admin_user_id>',
    'owner',
    NOW(),
    NOW()
FROM nc_bases_v2 b
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM nc_base_users_v2 bu
    WHERE bu.base_id = b.id AND bu.fk_user_id = '<super_admin_user_id>'
);
```

This is not ideal because:
- It must be done manually for every new base
- It defeats the purpose of having a Super Admin role
- It worked correctly in version 0.260.2

## Additional Context

### Debugging Steps Performed

1. **Cleared Redis cache** - Issue persisted
2. **Reset JWT token_version** - Issue persisted
3. **Disabled Redis entirely** - Issue persisted (confirmed not a caching issue)
4. **Attempted downgrade to 0.265.1** - Failed due to database schema migrations
5. **Tested v1 vs v2 API endpoints** - Identified v1 as the problem

### Downgrade Not Possible

Attempting to downgrade results in migration errors:
```
The migration directory is corrupt, the following files are missing: nc_092_composite_pk...
```

This means users who upgrade to 0.301.1 cannot easily roll back if they encounter this issue.



## Labels

- bug
- v1-api
- acl
- super-admin
- regression


`

### Desired Behavior

## Suggested Fix

The v1 API ACL middleware should check `nc_users_v2.roles` for the `super` role before returning 403, similar to how the v2 API endpoints handle authorization.

Relevant code likely in:
- `packages/nocodb/src/controllers/` - v1 API controllers
- `packages/nocodb/src/guards/` - ACL guards
- `packages/nocodb/src/middlewares/` - Authorization middleware

### Project Details

  NocoDB used as docker : true
  NocoDB version : 0.301.1
  Database used in NC_DB URL  : pg
  Project was created  by clicking : New Project
  Database on which spreadsheet is created : pg
  OS on which NocoDB is running : Linux (Docker on Linux 6.8.0-90-generic)
  Node.js version if running as node : 22.21.1 (inside Docker container)
  Database version :  PG 17 (external)


### Attachments

_No response_

nocodb/nocodb#12972.body:
## Steps to reproduce

```bash
curl -X PATCH "https://your-nocodb-instance/api/v2/meta/columns/{checkbox_column_id}" \
  -H "xc-auth: {your_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title": "is_draft", "description": "Test description"}'
```

**Result**: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
```json
{"msg":"Something didn't work as expected. Please try again. If the problem persists, contact support."}
```

## Desired Behavior

The API should successfully update the Checkbox column's description and return HTTP 200, as it does for other column types (Number, SingleLineText, Date, Decimal, etc.).

## Root Cause Analysis

I traced through the NocoDB source code and found the issue:

1. When PATCHing a column, `getColumnPropsFromUIDT` is called which adds default properties for the column type
2. For Checkbox columns, this sets `cdf = 'false'` ([PgUi.ts:1674-1677](https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb/blob/develop/packages/nocodb-sdk/src/lib/sqlUi/PgUi.ts#L1674-L1677))
3. This triggers `updateMetaAndDatabase` to run ALTER TABLE operations
4. These database operations fail for Checkbox columns, throwing an uncaught exception
5. The global exception filter catches this and returns the generic HTTP 500

The issue is that even with a minimal payload (`{"title": "...", "description": "..."}`), NocoDB's internal processing adds extra properties that trigger unnecessary database schema changes.

**Suggested fix**: Don't call `getColumnPropsFromUIDT` or `updateMetaAndDatabase` when only metadata fields (title/description) are being updated.

## Project Details

```
NocoDB used as docker: true
NocoDB version: 301.0.2
Database used in NC_DB URL: pg
Project was created by clicking: New Project by connecting to external database
Database on which spreadsheet is created: pg (Neon)
OS on which NocoDB is running: Linux (Docker)
```

## Additional Context

This was discovered while using the NocoDB R package to programmatically set column descriptions. Other column types (Number, SingleLineText, Date, Decimal, etc.) work correctly with the same minimal payload - only Checkbox fails.

nocodb/nocodb#13044.body:
## Bug report

Thank you for maintaining NocoDB – we're big fans and rely on it heavily for our project. We recently reported #12972 (Checkbox PATCH HTTP 500), which was promptly fixed in PR #12974. We really appreciate the quick turnaround! This new issue appears to be the same class of bug, but affecting `colOptions` instead of `cdf`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create or have a SingleSelect column with dropdown options configured
2. Send a PATCH request that only updates metadata (e.g. `description`):
   ```bash
   curl -X PATCH \
     'https://<nocodb>/api/v2/meta/columns/<column_id>' \
     -H 'xc-token: <token>' \
     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
     -d '{"description": "some description"}'
   ```
3. Check the column's `colOptions` – all dropdown options are gone

### Expected behavior

A PATCH that only sets `description` (or other metadata fields) should **not** affect `colOptions`. The existing dropdown options should be preserved.

### Actual behavior

All `colOptions.options` are silently wiped. The column remains a SingleSelect but with an empty options list. No error is returned – the API responds with 200.

### Likely root cause

PR #12974 fixed the same class of issue for `cdf`: `getColumnPropsFromUIDT` was overwriting `cdf` with defaults even when the PATCH body didn't include it. The same pattern likely applies to `colOptions` – when the PATCH body doesn't include `colOptions`, the column update code path still processes (and clears) the existing options.

### Environment

| Key | Value |
|---|---|
| NocoDB version | 0.301.2 |
| Hosting | Self-hosted on Fly.io |
| Database | External PostgreSQL (Neon), connected as external data source |
| API version | v2 (`/api/v2/meta/columns/{id}`) |

### Additional context

- The bug is reproducible with **any** PATCH payload on a SingleSelect column, not just `description`. Even `{"title": "same_title"}` wipes the options.
- This does not seem to affect columns on the default SQLite data source – we only observed it on an external PostgreSQL data source (though we haven't tested exhaustively).
- Related: #12972, PR #12974
nocodb/nocodb#13044.title: 🐛 Bug: PATCH on SingleSelect column wipes colOptions even for metadata-only updates

### Please confirm that the bug report does ***not*** already exist

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a table with ~700 rows
2. Add 2 Links columns (one m2m, one has-many) and 3 Lookup columns through those links
3. Open the grid view with all columns visible, page size = 100
4. Observe 13+ second load time
5. Hide all Links/Lookup columns → loads in ~0.1s

Timings measured inside the container (no network overhead):
- Plain fields only: **0.1s**
- With linked columns, limit=25: **3.3s**
- With linked columns, limit=100: **13s**

Time scales linearly with row count, consistent with N+1 query pattern — each row triggers separate queries per linked column rather than batch loading.

Tested on both SQLite and PostgreSQL 17.4 with identical results.

### Desired Behavior

Linked records for a page of rows should be fetched in batch using `WHERE id IN (...)` — O(link_columns) queries total, not O(rows × link_columns). Loading 100 rows with a few linked columns should take <1 second.
